page: OVHcloud b3-32 is a general-purpose cloud server powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs with a single thread per core, operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 3077.97 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1888.0, both placing in the top 25%. Memory write performance is also strong, with large block uncached write bandwidth reaching 96.92 GB/sec, complemented by a strong PassMark memory score of 2793.86. Multi-core performance is average, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 9677.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 20381.49. Text processing and small-model LLM text generation also demonstrate strong performance, while database operations, Redis SET throughput, and static web serving fall within average ranges.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offer reliable resource density without the performance degradation associated with shared-core environments. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage and a dedicated public IP address enhances the value profile for sustained workloads. This server is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, text processing, web serving, and moderate database operations.

Multi-core performance, database operations, and static web serving throughput fall within average ranges. With dedicated CPU allocation and a balanced resource profile, this server offers stable performance for general-purpose applications, software compilation, text processing, and moderate database workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores, local NVMe storage, and strong single-core processing capabilities.

32 GB system memory and 200 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and memory write benchmark performance
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Suitable for general-purpose applications, text processing, and software compilation
